grammar.title: Causal ‘ya que’ + indicative
grammar.detail: ‘ya que’ states a known cause / premise: ‘since you (in fact) have been raised…’. Always takes the indicative.

grammar.title: Imperative ‘busquen’
grammar.detail: ustedes-imperative of buscar. Spelling adjustment: c → qu before -e so the hard /k/ sound is preserved (busque, busquen).

expression.phrase: Cristo sentado a la derecha de Dios
expression.note: Echoes Psalm 110:1. The seated posture matters — Jesus’ work is finished; he reigns from a throne, not a battlefield.

grammar.title: Imperative ‘Concentren’
grammar.detail: ustedes-imperative of -ar verb concentrar. The negative version would be ‘no concentren…’ (same form).

grammar.title: Ellipsis ‘no en las de la tierra’
grammar.detail: Spanish freely drops a repeated noun: ‘no en las (cosas) de la tierra’. The article ‘las’ alone carries the noun.

expression.phrase: Concentren su atención en las cosas de arriba
expression.note: Not escape from the world, but reorientation of focus. The values of the resurrection world set the agenda for life here.

grammar.title: Present perfect ‘han muerto’
grammar.detail: ‘you have died’ — perfect tense uses haber + past participle. ‘muerto’ is the irregular p.p. of morir (cf. abierto, escrito, dicho).

grammar.title: estar + p.p. (state of being hidden)
grammar.detail: ‘su vida está escondida’ — estar focuses on the current state. The action of hiding is past and complete; the state continues.

expression.phrase: su vida está escondida con Cristo en Dios
expression.note: Two prepositions layered: ‘with Christ’ and ‘in God’. The believer’s true identity is invisible from the outside — its safety lies in the divine container.
